spring_boot_best_practices.overview: Best practices and conventions for building enterprise-grade Spring Boot applications during the modernization process. These guidelines ensure code quality, maintainability, and adherence to Spring Boot standards.

principle.name: Package Organization by Layer
principle.description: Organize code by architectural layers, not by features
principle.structure: com.abcbank/ ├── controller/ # Presentation layer │ ├── AccountController.java │ ├── TransactionController.java │ └── TransferController.java ├── service/ # Business logic layer │ ├── AccountService.java │ ├── TransactionService.java │ └── TransferService.java ├── repository/ # Data access layer │ ├── AccountRepository.java │ ├── TransactionRepository.java │ └── UserRepository.java ├── model/ # Domain entities │ ├── Account.java │ ├── Transaction.java │ └── User.java ├── dto/ # Data Transfer Objects │ ├── request/ │ │ ├── LoginRequest.java │ │ └── TransferRequest.java │ └── response/ │ ├── AccountResponse.java │ └── TransferResponse.java ├── config/ # Configuration classes │ ├── SecurityConfig.java │ └── DatabaseConfig.java ├── exception/ # Custom exceptions │ ├── AccountNotFoundException.java │ ├── InsufficientFundsException.java │ └── GlobalExceptionHandler.java └── AbcBankApplication.java

principle.name: Use Spring Boot Starter Dependencies
principle.description: Leverage Spring Boot starters for consistent dependency versions
principle.required_dependencies:
<dependencies> <!-- Spring Boot Starters --> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-security</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-actuator</artifactId> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId> </dependency>
<!-- Thymeleaf (if server-side rendering) --> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-thymeleaf</artifactId> </dependency>
<!-- Database --> <dependency> <groupId>org.postgresql</groupId> <artifactId>postgresql</artifactId> <scope>runtime</scope> </dependency>
<!-- Utilities --> <dependency> <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId> <artifactId>lombok</artifactId> <optional>true</optional> </dependency>
<!-- Testing --> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId> <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId> <scope>test</scope> </dependency> <dependency> <groupId>org.springframework.security</groupId> <artifactId>spring-security-test</artifactId> <scope>test</scope> </dependency> </dependencies>

principle.name: Java 17 Configuration
principle.description: Configure Maven for Java 17 and Spring Boot 3.x

principle.name: Proper JPA Entity Annotations
principle.description: Use appropriate JPA annotations for entity mapping

principle.name: Use BigDecimal for Money
principle.description: Always use BigDecimal for monetary values, never float or double
principle.rationale: Floating-point arithmetic can introduce rounding errors in financial calculations
principle.bad: private double balance; // NEVER use double for money!

principle.name: Implement equals() and hashCode()
principle.description: Properly implement equals and hashCode for entities
principle.note: Or use Lombok's @EqualsAndHashCode(onlyExplicitlyIncluded = true) with @EqualsAndHashCode.Include on business key

principle.name: Use Spring Data JPA Repositories
principle.description: Leverage Spring Data JPA for data access

principle.name: Avoid N+1 Query Problems
principle.description: Use JOIN FETCH or EntityGraph to avoid N+1 queries

principle.name: Use @Transactional Appropriately
principle.description: Apply @Transactional at the service layer for business operations

guidelines.guideline: Place @Transactional on service methods, not repository methods
Use readOnly = true for read-only operations
Specify rollbackFor for checked exceptions if needed
Keep transactions as short as possible

principle.name: Use DTOs for Data Transfer
principle.description: Never expose entities directly in controllers - use DTOs
principle.rationale: DTOs provide a stable API contract, prevent over-fetching, and avoid lazy loading issues

principle.name: Implement Proper Logging
principle.description: Use SLF4J with appropriate log levels

guidelines.guideline: Log at appropriate levels (ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE)

principle.name: RESTful Controller Design
principle.description: Follow REST conventions for API endpoints

principle.name: Input Validation
principle.description: Validate all input using Bean Validation annotations

principle.name: Global Exception Handling
principle.description: Use @ControllerAdvice for centralized exception handling

principle.name: Spring Security Configuration
principle.description: Configure Spring Security for authentication and authorization

principle.name: Password Hashing
principle.description: Always hash passwords using BCrypt

principle.name: Externalize Configuration
principle.description: Use application.yml for configuration with environment variable support
principle.example:
# application.yml spring: application: name: abc-bank
datasource: url: ${SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL:jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/abcbank} username: ${SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME:abcbank} password: ${SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD:changeme} driver-class-name: org.postgresql.Driver hikari: maximum-pool-size: 10 minimum-idle: 5 connection-timeout: 30000
jpa: hibernate: ddl-auto: ${SPRING_JPA_HIBERNATE_DDL_AUTO:update} show-sql: ${SPRING_JPA_SHOW_SQL:false} properties: hibernate: dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect format_sql: true use_sql_comments: true
sql: init: mode: ${SPRING_SQL_INIT_MODE:always} data-locations: classpath:data.sql
security: user: name: ${ADMIN_USERNAME:admin} password: ${ADMIN_PASSWORD:admin}
server: port: ${SERVER_PORT:8080} servlet: context-path: / error: include-message: always include-binding-errors: always
management: endpoints: web: exposure: include: health,info,metrics endpoint: health: show-details: when-authorized probes: enabled: true health: livenessState: enabled: true readinessState: enabled: true
logging: level: root: INFO com.abcbank: ${LOG_LEVEL:DEBUG} org.springframework.web: INFO org.hibernate.SQL: ${SQL_LOG_LEVEL:DEBUG} org.hibernate.type.descriptor.sql.BasicBinder: TRACE pattern: console: "%d{yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss} - %msg%n"
info: app: name: ABC Bank description: Modernized Banking Application version: 2.0.0 encoding: ${project.build.sourceEncoding} java: version: ${java.version}

principle.name: Profile-Specific Configuration
principle.description: Use Spring profiles for environment-specific settings
principle.example:
# application-dev.yml spring: jpa: show-sql: true hibernate: ddl-auto: create-drop sql: init: mode: always
logging: level: com.abcbank: DEBUG org.hibernate.SQL: DEBUG
# application-prod.yml spring: jpa: show-sql: false hibernate: ddl-auto: validate sql: init: mode: never
logging: level: com.abcbank: INFO org.hibernate.SQL: WARN

principle.name: Unit Testing Services
principle.description: Write unit tests for service layer logic

principle.name: Integration Testing Controllers
principle.description: Use @SpringBootTest for integration tests

pitfall.name: Exposing Entities in REST APIs
pitfall.problem: Directly returning JPA entities from controllers
pitfall.why_bad: - Exposes internal structure - Can cause lazy loading exceptions - Tight coupling between API and database - Security risks (exposing sensitive fields)
pitfall.solution: Always use DTOs for API responses

pitfall.name: Not Using @Transactional
pitfall.why_bad: - No transaction management - Lazy loading exceptions - Data inconsistency

pitfall.name: Using float/double for Money
pitfall.problem: Using floating-point types for monetary values

pitfall.name: Hardcoding Configuration
pitfall.why_bad: - Not cloud-native - Security risk - Difficult to deploy across environments

pitfall.name: Not Implementing Health Checks
pitfall.solution: Enable Spring Boot Actuator with health endpoints
